bids.reasoning: At the opening (t=0.0), I am targeting my maximum-utility bid because there is no time pressure to concede yet. There are no opponent offers or messages to infer their priorities, so I will start with my best values on all issues, including issueD where I am indifferent due to zero weight.

I am targeting a near-maximal utility bid because it is extremely early (t=0.02) and there is no need to make meaningful concessions yet. The opponent’s first offer gives me moderate value mainly through issueE, but it asks concessions on highly important issueF and issueB, so I will hold firm there while changing only issueD, which has zero weight for me, to avoid repeating my exact prior bid.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still extremely early at t=0.04, so there is little reason to concede on my important issues yet. The opponent has kept issueE at my preferred valueA but is offering weak values on my highest-weight issueF and other issues, so I will hold firm on issueF, issueB, issueC, and issueA while varying the zero-weight issueD.

At t=0.06 it is still very early, so I am targeting near-maximal utility and only conceding on issueD, which has zero importance to me. The opponent has consistently offered issueE=valueA, which is also my top value, but has not improved on my high-weight issueF and issueB, so I should hold firm there rather than concede prematurely.

At t=0.08 it is still very early, so I am targeting a near-maximal utility bid and only conceding on issueD, which has zero weight for me. The opponent’s offers remain low for me and show little real concession, though they consistently give issueE: valueA, so I will hold firm on my high-importance issues B, E, and F.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting my maximum substantive utility while only varying the zero-weight issueD to avoid repeating my last bid. The opponent’s offers have mostly stayed low for me, though their latest move on issueF from valueA to valueB is a small concession; I will hold firm on high-weight issueF, issueB, and issueE for now.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.87 by conceding on issueA, which is relatively low weight for me and appears repeatedly important to the opponent. Since time is still early at t=0.12 and the opponent has mostly held firm with only a brief concession on issueF, I will keep my strongest positions on high-weight issues B, E, and F while varying the bid to avoid repeating my last offer.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.866 because it is still early at t=0.14, so there is no need for major concessions yet. The opponent has started conceding on my most important issueF by offering valueC, while still holding issueB and issueC away from my top values, so I will hold firm on issueB, issueC, issueE, and issueF and concede on lower/zero-weight issueA and issueD.

I am targeting a strong early-game utility around 0.81, since time is still only at 0.16 and there is no need to make deep concessions yet. The opponent has consistently offered issueE: valueA and has occasionally conceded on issueF, but they keep pushing issueB: valueB and issueC: valueA; I will hold firm on high-weight issueB and issueF while conceding on lower-weight issueA, issueC, and the zero-weight issueD.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.77 while making concessions on lower-weight issues A and C that the opponent has repeatedly pushed toward valueB/valueA. Time is still early at 0.18, so I should hold firm on my most important issues, especially issueF=valueC and issueB=valueC, while keeping issueE=valueA since both sides have consistently used it. The opponent’s offers are not steadily improving for me, so I will not make a major concession yet.

I am targeting a utility around 0.74, which is a moderate concession from my earlier high-utility bids while still protecting my most important issueF and issueB. At t=0.20 there is still time, so I concede on lower-weight issueC and irrelevant issueD in line with the opponent’s latest offer, while holding issueB at valueC. The opponent has shown willingness to give issueF: valueC and issueE: valueA, with no messages to reinterpret their bidding behavior.

I am targeting a strong weighted utility of about 0.81, since it is still early at t=0.22 and there is no need for major concessions yet. The opponent consistently accepts issueE=valueA and has shown flexibility on issueA, issueC, and issueD, so I concede on lower-weight issues while holding firm on high-weight issueB and issueF.

I am targeting a utility around 0.77, conceding on lower-weight issueA and issueC while holding firm on the high-value issueF, issueE, and issueB because it is still relatively early at t=0.24. The opponent has consistently offered issueE: valueA and has recently conceded issueF to valueC, but they keep pushing issueB: valueB and weaker values on issueA/issueC, so I am using issueA/issueC/D as tradeoffs while preserving my core value.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.74 by holding firm on my highest-value issues issueF=valueC, issueE=valueA, and issueB=valueC while conceding lower-weight issues. At t=0.26 it is too early to give up issueB, but the opponent has repeatedly signaled acceptance of issueA=valueB/valueA, issueC=valueA, issueD=valueB, issueE=valueA, and recently issueF=valueC, so this bid moves toward their pattern without collapsing my payoff.

At t=0.28, there is still time, so I am targeting a strong utility bid for me, about 0.814, rather than making a late-stage concession. The opponent consistently accepts issueE: valueA and often pushes issueB: valueB, while intermittently conceding issueF to valueC; I will hold firm on my high-weight issueF and issueB, while conceding lower-weight issueA, issueC, and zero-weight issueD toward values they have shown interest in.

I am targeting a solid mid-high utility around 0.79, holding firm on my most important issues issueF=valueC, issueE=valueA, and issueB=valueC while conceding lower-weight issueA and issueC toward values the opponent has repeatedly offered. At t=0.30 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept their lower-utility pattern; their offers show movement on issueF to valueC and consistent agreement on issueE=valueA, but they still resist issueB=valueC. No opponent messages were provided, so I am relying only on the bid history.

I am targeting a utility around 0.74, conceding on lower-weight issues issueA and issueC while holding firm on the high-value issues issueB, issueE, and issueF. At t=0.32 there is still time, but the opponent has increasingly offered issueF: valueC and consistently allows issueE: valueA, so I am using that signal while making a more accommodating bid than my prior one.

I am targeting a moderate-utility bid of about 0.68, conceding on lower-weight issues A/C/D and partially on issueB while holding my strongest priorities issueF=valueC and issueE=valueA. At t=0.34 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept the opponent’s low issueB/issueC positions fully; their offers show repeated willingness to give me issueE=valueA and increasingly issueF=valueC, with issueB remaining the main conflict. No opponent messages were provided, so I rely only on the offer pattern.

At t=0.36 I am targeting a moderate-utility bid around 0.64, conceding on lower/mid-weight issues while holding issueF=valueC and issueE=valueA because they drive much of my utility. The opponent has repeatedly offered issueE=valueA and sometimes issueF=valueC, but their latest moved issueF back to valueB, so I will counter by preserving valueC on issueF while matching much of their apparent pattern elsewhere. No natural-language messages were provided, so I rely only on the valid offer history.

I am targeting a solid utility of about 0.684, which is below my maximum but still strong at t=0.38. The opponent has steadily signaled willingness to give me issueE=valueA and recently issueF=valueC, while resisting my best values on issueB and issueC, so I will preserve the high-value F/E combination and accept their latest concessions rather than overpush mid-game.
